# Auto-generated walkthrough guide

## Winter

> look

# Dark and skeletal against the white sky, creaking softly in the wind.
> examine branches
# Pristine and endless, glittering faintly where light touches it.
> examine snow

## Spring

> south
> look

# Purple and white, fragile but stubborn - the first to arrive, every year.
> examine crocuses
# You can't see the birds, but their song is everywhere - layered and
#   intricate, as though the whole canopy were singing.
> examine birdsong

## Summer

> south
> look

# It sways at waist height, seed-heavy and golden at the tips.
> examine grass
# It falls in bright columns through the leaf canopy, thick and almost
#   tangible.
> examine sunlight

## Fall

> south
> look

# They spin slowly as they descend - oak, maple, birch - each one catching the
#   light before it settles.
> examine leaves

## Winter

> south
> look
